text: 100g of fat. polyunsaturated fat is one of the better parts of the profile. for this category, the real question is fat quality.
0g of carbs. the rest of the section is nothing special. not relevant here.
0g of protein. everything else here is pretty flat. also not relevant here.
vitamin k at 11% daily value, vitamin e at 1% daily value, vitamin a at 0% daily value stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side: highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

the drawbacks next: extremely calorie dense by default because that is the unavoidable category burden. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal.
A tier. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
